mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 5.25% signals a tightening monetary cycle that raises equity discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.25%, or the Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.7% indicates a tight labor market that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.7%, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1,795 thousand units signals strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1795.0, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and payrolls indicates robust economic activity, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to generate positive 20d returns in > 60% of cases, or the joint Rank IC drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP above 11,397.6 billion dollars reflects sustained economic growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 exceeds 11,397.6, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production above 113.7063 signals manufacturing strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 113.7063, or the Rank IC drops below 0.03 over 1-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the CPI above 203.7 signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 203.7, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During high unemployment regimes, rising consumer confidence (RSAFS) correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic expectations improve.
failure_condition: The interaction between UNRATE and RSAFS fails to generate positive 20d returns in > 60% of cases during high unemployment, or the joint Rank IC drops below 0.03.
